summary: Officially documented TDR hang-count reset. This app write restores TdrLimitCount to Microsoft's documented default of 5, but Microsoft warns that TDR keys are intended for driver testing and debugging rather than normal end-user tuning.

setting.name: Graphics TDR Limit Count
setting.casual_explanation: Windows keeps track of how many GPU hangs occur within the TDR time window. This setting controls that count threshold.
setting.why_people_change_it: People usually touch it only when undoing manual TDR debugging changes or restoring graphics-driver timeout behavior to the documented baseline.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Restoring the documented default helps undo manual driver experiments, but Microsoft does not present this as a general-user performance setting.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents TdrLimitCount under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers and states that the default value is 5. The same Microsoft page warns that TDR keys are for testing or debugging during driver development only.

allowed_values.label: Microsoft default hang count
allowed_values.meaning: The TDR hang-count threshold is set to Microsoft's documented default of 5.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes TdrLimitCount = 5 on the documented GraphicsDrivers path.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems restoring the documented TDR hang-count baseline

states.rationale: Microsoft documents 5 as the default TdrLimitCount value.

windows_defaults.notes: This record exists to document and restore the default baseline rather than to encourage casual TDR tuning.

recommended_profiles.label: Reset to Microsoft default
intended_for: Systems returning to the documented baseline

states.rationale: Restore the documented default hang-count threshold.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are removing custom TDR edits and want the Microsoft baseline back.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are treating TDR thresholds as normal consumer tuning.
tradeoffs: The documented Microsoft default is restored.
The setting remains a driver-debugging surface rather than a general-user recommendation.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Testing and debugging TDR during driver development
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents TdrLimitCount under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers and states that the default value is 5, while warning that TDR keys are for targeted driver testing and debugging only.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes TdrLimitCount = 5 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact path and default value, and the app writes that documented default as a reset-to-baseline action with a clean rollback story.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: KeyPath : HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers; KeyValue : TdrLimitCount; ValueType : REG_DWORD; ValueData : Number of TDRs before crashing. The default value is 5.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft's TDR registry keys page documents the exact GraphicsDrivers runtime path, the TdrLimitCount value name, and the documented default of 5.
